title: Cultural Shifts in a Professional Learning Community
book: Revisiting Professional Learning Communities at Work, 2nd Edition
description: Names the mindset shifts that define a real PLC — from a focus on teaching to a focus on learning, from isolation to collaboration, and from "my kids" to "our kids."
howToUse: Use as the opening culture-setting document (Meeting 1). Have teams highlight where they currently operate and circle the shifts EMS is working toward this year.

title: Developing Team Norms
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A structured process for teams to create 4–6 behavior-based norms — not generic statements like "be respectful," but commitments that protect the work when it gets uncomfortable.
howToUse: Use before teams start content work (Meeting 2). Each PLT drafts norms it will actually hold itself to, especially when looking at shared student evidence.

title: Establishing Team Roles
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Clarifies who facilitates, records notes, watches time, brings data, and follows up — so meetings produce something instead of drifting into informal conversation.
howToUse: Use alongside team norms (Meeting 2). Assign facilitator, recorder, timekeeper, and data lead, and revisit as needed.

title: Critical Issues for Team Consideration
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A team self-assessment covering norms, SMART goals, essential learnings, common assessments, proficiency criteria, use of results, and intervention. Helps teams name their current reality.
howToUse: Use as a baseline check (Meeting 3), then revisit at midyear and end-of-year (Meeting 18). A good, non-punitive Guiding Coalition monitoring tool.

title: Protocol for Reviewing Team Norms
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A short routine for revisiting and reaffirming norms once a team has been working together for a few meetings.
howToUse: Use in the first buffer meeting (Meeting 4) to reset culture and clear up confusion before the learning-cycle work begins.

title: Using REAL to Identify Essential Standards
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Helps teams decide what is truly essential using four filters: Readiness, Endurance, Assessed, and Leverage. Especially useful for teams that want to teach everything equally.
howToUse: Use when selecting the one essential standard for this year's cycle (Meeting 5).

title: Deconstructing an Essential Standard
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Breaks a standard into what students must know, understand, and be able to do — the bridge from a broad standard to teachable, assessable learning targets.
howToUse: Use right after a team identifies its essential standard (Meetings 5 and 6).

title: Standards Unpacking Template
book: The 15-Day Challenge
description: A template for turning standards into learning targets and student-friendly "I can" statements.
howToUse: Use after essential standards are selected (Meeting 6). Pairs directly with the EMS Learning Target Breakdown.

title: Gaining Shared Clarity
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Helps a team reach genuine agreement on what proficiency looks like — so the same assessment isn't scored through different lenses.
howToUse: Use when defining proficiency (Meeting 7) and revisit to calibrate before designing the assessment (Meeting 8).

title: Developing a Success Checklist for a Learning Intention
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A template for building clear success criteria for each learning target.
howToUse: Use to make proficiency concrete (Meetings 7 and 8).

title: Building a Common Formative Assessment
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Keeps teams focused on aligning a common assessment to essential standards, learning targets, rigor, and agreed proficiency — designed to inform instruction, not just generate a grade.
howToUse: Use before teams create or revise the CFA (Meeting 9).

title: Using the ACID Test to Evaluate a Common Formative Assessment
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A quality-control check applied after the CFA is drafted. Prevents a "common quiz" from being mistaken for a true common formative assessment.
howToUse: Use to pressure-test the draft assessment before giving it (Meeting 10).

title: Actionable Data Tool
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A simple structure for recording results so the team can plan how data will be captured and acted on — by student and by target.
howToUse: Use to plan data collection (Meeting 10) and again when reviewing reassessment results (Meeting 17).

title: Pacing Guide for a Cycle of Instruction
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Maps out the instruction-teach-assess window for the learning cycle so the team teaches with the end in mind.
howToUse: Use when planning instruction before the CFA (Meeting 11) and revisit during the mid-cycle check (Meeting 12).

title: Prerequisite Planning Document
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Helps teams surface the prerequisite skills and knowledge students need before tackling the essential standard.
howToUse: Use during instructional planning (Meeting 11) to anticipate gaps before they show up on the assessment.

title: List of Common Misconceptions for an Essential Outcome
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A place to record the misconceptions students commonly hold about the essential outcome, so teams can teach and check for them proactively.
howToUse: Use during the mid-cycle troubleshooting meeting (Meeting 12) to adjust instruction while learning is still happening.

title: Team Protocol for Analyzing Assessment Results
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: One of the most important tools: walks teams through "What? So what? Now what?" and pushes them toward intervention, extension, and teacher learning.
howToUse: Use every time a team brings CFA data (Meeting 13), when discussing teacher learning (Meeting 14), and again at reassessment (Meeting 17).

title: Item-Analysis Sheet Template
book: The 15-Day Challenge
description: A simple way to record item-by-item or target-by-target results so teams see patterns instead of only total scores.
howToUse: Use during the CFA data review (Meeting 13).

title: Evidence of Practice in Action — Teacher Self-Reflection
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Keeps the focus on teacher learning, not just student deficits. Teams ask, "What did we learn instructionally?"
howToUse: Use after the data review, before planning intervention (Meeting 14).

title: Team-Based Intervention Plan for Struggling Learners
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Moves a team from "these students didn't get it" to "here is what we're doing, who's doing it, and how we'll know if it worked."
howToUse: Use to plan a timely, targeted, directive response after data analysis (Meeting 15) and revisit to monitor it (Meeting 16).

title: Targeting Tier 2 Interventions for an Individual Student
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A more individualized support plan for a student who needs more than the first reteach/reassess cycle.
howToUse: Use as needed when a student requires support beyond the team's first intervention round.

title: Creating a Tiered Task Card to Extend Student Learning
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Helps teams design deeper learning for students who are already proficient — so extension is deeper work, not just more work.
howToUse: Use to plan extension alongside intervention (Meeting 15) and revisit to monitor it (Meeting 16).

title: Team Flexible Time Planning Prompts
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Prompts for planning how flexible/intervention time will actually be used across the team.
howToUse: Optional support during the intervention/extension check (Meeting 16).

title: Learning Team Quarterly Reflection
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: A gentle, non-punitive reflection on team progress. Good for closing a cycle and for Guiding Coalition monitoring.
howToUse: Use to close the year and plan the next cycle (Meeting 18), and once per quarter with each PLT.

title: Team SMART Goal Template
book: The Collaborative Team Plan Book for PLCs at Work
description: A structure for writing a measurable student-learning goal once a team has baseline data.
howToUse: Use once teams understand essential standards and CFAs — not before. Layer it in when a team is ready for a measurable goal.

title: Glossary of Key Terms and Concepts
book: The Handbook for Highly Effective Teams in a PLC at Work
description: Shared definitions for terms like essential learning, common formative assessment, intervention, extension, team norms, and continuous improvement.
howToUse: Keep in the appendix and reference whenever teams are still building shared vocabulary.

title: EMS Template: Team Commitments
description: EMS-ready team commitments, roles, and meeting logistics — an editable equivalent inspired by the PLC at Work process.
howToUse: Complete in Step 1 / Meeting 2 as your team's foundation document.

title: EMS Template: Essential Standards Organizer
description: Organizes each essential standard into what students must know, do, the academic vocabulary, and what proficiency looks like.
howToUse: Complete in Step 2 / Meeting 5 as you identify your essential standard.

title: EMS Template: Learning Target Breakdown
description: Breaks an essential standard into learning targets with student-friendly "I can" statements and how each will be taught and checked.
howToUse: Complete in Step 3 / Meeting 6.

title: EMS Template: Common Formative Assessment Planner
description: Aligns each assessment item to a learning target, rigor level, and the agreed proficient response, with data-review dates.
howToUse: Complete in Step 4 / Meetings 9–10.

title: EMS Template: Student Learning Evidence Protocol
description: Sorts students by learning target into proficient, not-yet-proficient, and ready-for-extension, with patterns and notes.
howToUse: Complete in Step 5 / Meeting 13.

title: EMS Template: Intervention Planning Tool
description: Plans a timely, targeted response by student: the specific need, strategy, who supports, when, and reassessment evidence.
howToUse: Complete in Step 6 / Meeting 15.

title: EMS Template: Extension Planning Tool
description: Plans deeper learning for proficient students: evidence of proficiency, the extension task, and the depth/application required.
howToUse: Complete in Step 7 / Meeting 15.

title: EMS Template: Teacher Learning Reflection
description: Captures what worked, the evidence for it, who can share the strategy, and what the team will try next.
howToUse: Complete in Step 8 / Meeting 14.
